member.office_address: 2423 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0528

form_analysis.form_header_text: Rep. Judy Chu FY2027 Programmatic and Language Appropriations Requests
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Submit this form if you have programmatic or language requests for FY27. The deadline to submit to Congresswoman Chu is Sunday, March 8, 2026, by 11:59m PT. DO NOT submit this form for Community Project Funding requests. Our form for FY27 CPF requests is here: https://forms.office.com/g/fSMJHnVuu5

sections.description: Please provide information for the entity requesting funds and the primary point of contact.

fields.label: Point of Contact Name and Title
Are you located in CA-28? https://www.house.gov/representatives/find-your-representative
fields.help_text: Verify your district using the provided link.

sections.description: Provide the technical details of your programmatic or language request.

fields.label: Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies (CJS) subcommittee, as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values explicitly list the agency as NASA.

fields.label: Bureau
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values list the Account Full Name as Science Mission Directorate, which serves as the bureau-level entity for this request.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values explicitly list the account as 'Science'.

fields.label: Request Title
fields.rationale: Standard title based on the Short Title in the reference values.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: This request is for a specific funding amount for an existing agency program (SMD), which is classified as a programmatic request.

fields.label: Amount Requested
fields.rationale: This is the exact funding ask provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Request Description
fields.draft_value: This request provides $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to ensure U.S. leadership in space exploration and protect critical workforce stability. In CA-28, NASA Science has invested over $6.3 billion recently, supporting over 390 contracts at the California Institute of Technology for vital missions like Europa Clipper, Mars Sample Return, and NEO Surveyor. This funding restores stability following FY2026 disruptions, sustaining the talent pipeline and economic returns for the district and the nation.
fields.rationale: Combines the $9 billion ask with specific local spending data for CA-28 ($6.3B total, 390+ contracts at Caltech) and highlights key local projects like Europa Clipper and Mars Sample Return mentioned in the spending context.

fields.label: Language Type
fields.rationale: The request type is 'Programmatic' and the reference values indicate no report or bill language is being requested for this specific submission.

fields.label: Language
fields.help_text: Provide the specific text for the language request.
fields.rationale: The request context explicitly states that no report or bill language is requested (N/A).

fields.label: Briefly describe how this request will benefit CA-28
fields.draft_value:
* Supports over $1.9 billion in annual NASA Science obligations within CA-28, fueling local economic growth and high-tech innovation.
* Sustains critical leadership roles at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) for flagship missions including Europa Clipper, Mars Sample Return, and the Roman Space Telescope.
* Secures the specialized aerospace workforce at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) for deep space network management and planetary defense projects like the NEO Surveyor.
* Strengthens the local STEM pipeline and research infrastructure through over $70 million in active grants to Pasadena-based institutions.
fields.rationale: CA-28 is home to NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory and Caltech, which received over $6.3 billion in NASA Science obligations between FY2023 and FY2025. The draft highlights major active contracts for Europa Clipper ($2.9B), Mars Sample Return ($1.59B), and NEO Surveyor ($665M) as listed in the district spending data.

top_contracts.description: Europa clipper project  the contract is the sponsoring agreement between the national aeronautics and space administration NASA and the california institute of technology-contractor, a private nonprofit educational institution, which establishes the relationship for the operation of the federally funded research and development center-ffrdc known as the jet propulsion laboratory. The contractor, JPL, is required to perform work that is designated in task orders issued by the NASA management office-nmo contracting officers. Nasa-sponsored work: jpls primary mission is to support the NASA science mission directorate-smd in carrying out the specific objectives identified in the smd science plan.  The four broad scientific areas are: Earth science, planetary science, heliophysics, and astrophysics.  In performance of this contract, JPL shall: 1. Supply a broad base of scientific and technical capabilities relevant to NASA program and project responsibilities and assignments. 2. Foster its unique relationship with a top-tier university to facilitate the involvement of scientists, engineers, and students from the university and research communities in NASA mission and in supporting other government agencies. 3. Support NASA in enabling program and institutional capabilities. 4. Develop spacecraft and instruments that are sent to various destinations with our solar system, including Earth orbit, planets, planetary satellites, asteroids, and comets.  These missions may be orbiters, landers, or rovers, and typically include cutting-edge technologies required to meet scientific requirements. 5. Perform project tasks involving: i-autonomous deep-space, inner-space and earth-orbiting spacecraft or major subsystems, ii-experiments, instruments, or other devices which may be carried as payload on spacecrafts in missions managed by others, and-or iii-ground-based systems. 6. Beyond its primary mission, JPL may perform work for other NASA mission directorates or offices. 7. JPL is also responsible for the operation, research, technology insertion, and management of nasas deep space network to provide telecommunication and operation services, including data acquisition and data delivery required to meet established agency objectives. 8. JPL is also required to maintain and conduct an education program in close coordination with the NASA headquarters education office, the academic community at large, and in support to NASA strategic objectives to improve student retention in the science, technology, engineering, and mathematics-stem disciplines. Work for non-nasa sponsors:  JPL may perform work for non-nasa sponsors.  This work will be designated in task orders issued by nmo contracting officers based on task plans initiated by the contractor.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the Europa Clipper spacecraft for ocean world exploration.

top_contracts.description: Psyche: journey to a metal world  the contract is the sponsoring agreement between the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and the california institute of Technology (contractor), a private nonprofit educational institution, which establishes the relationship for the operation of the Federally Funded Research and Development Center (FFRDC) known as the jet propulsion laboratory. The contractor, JPL, is required to perform work that is designated in task orders issued by the Nasa Management Office (NMO) contracting officers.   Nasa - sponsored work: JPL'S primary Mission is to support the Nasa Science Mission Directorate (SMD) in carrying out the specific objectives identified in the smd Science plan.  The four broad scientific areas are: Earth Science, planetary Science, heliophysics, and astrophysics.  In performance of this contract, JPL shall:   1) supply a broad base of scientific and technical capabilities relevant to Nasa program and project responsibilities and assignments. 2) foster its unique relationship with a top-tier university to facilitate the involvement of scientists, engineers, and students from the university and Research communities in Nasa Mission and in supporting other government agencies. 3) support Nasa in enabling program and institutional capabilities. 4) develop spacecraft and instruments that are sent to various destinations with our solar system, including Earth orbit, planets, planetary satellites, asteroids, and comets.  These missions may be orbiters, landers, or rovers, and typically include cutting-edge technologies required to meet scientific requirements. 5) perform project tasks Involving: (I) autonomous deep-space, inner-space and earth-orbiting spacecraft or major subsystems, (II) experiments, instruments, or other devices which may be carried as payload on spacecrafts in missions managed by others, and/or (III) ground-based systems. 6) beyond its primary Mission, JPL may perform work for other Nasa Mission directorates or offices. 7) JPL is also responsible for the operation, Research, Technology insertion, and Management of Nasa S deep Space network to provide telecommunication and operation services, including data acquisition and data delivery required to meet established agency objectives. 8) JPL is also required to maintain and conduct an education program in close coordination with the Nasa headquarters education Office, the academic community at large, and in support to Nasa strategic objectives to improve student retention in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) disciplines.   Work for non-nasa sponsors:  JPL may perform work for non-nasa sponsors.  This work will be designated in task orders issued by nmo contracting officers based on task plans initiated by the contractor.

top_grants.generated_award_summary: Generating rapid alerts for new discoveries from the Roman Telescope.

top_grants.description: Fireball-2: the next generation of uv science  technology  and leadership
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Advancing ultraviolet space science with Fireball-2 technology.

top_grants.description: During fy25-30  the volume of irsa'S holdings will grow by an order of magnitude (SEC.2) as we ingest data from euclid  spherex  neorveyor  ultrasat  and ariel. These new missions will be transformational in all science areas highlighted in the 2020 decadal survey report. Irsa will also continue to release data from neowise  irtf  and the community  resources which have a strong track record of enabling novel research. We will also continue our successful initiative to provide public access to simulated data necessary for the success of the next generation of cosmology surveys.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Providing public access to massive new cosmic survey data archives.

top_grants.description: The goal of this opportunity is to fund archives that make scientific data from NASA astrophysics flight missions and nasa-funded researchers available to the us community  or that hold bibliographic and catalog information related to those data.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting archives for accessible NASA astrophysics scientific data.

top_grants.description: Research activities associated with the nustar legacy survey program. This includes reduction and publication of the on-going swift bat legacy survey. 2) management and leadership of the nustar science team through partial salary support for PI harri
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Publishing legacy X-ray survey results from NuSTAR and Swift observatories.

top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing ultrasensitive far-infrared space sensor arrays.
